title: C Preprocessor
content:
### C Preprocessor

The preprocessor is a text-processing tool that runs before the compiler. All preprocessor directives start with `#`.

### Common Directives

| Directive | Purpose | Example |
|-----------|---------|---------|
| `#include` | Insert file contents | `#include <stdio.h>` |
| `#define` | Define macro | `#define MAX 100` |
| `#undef` | Remove macro | `#undef MAX` |
| `#ifdef` | If macro defined | `#ifdef DEBUG` |
| `#ifndef` | If macro not defined | `#ifndef HEADER_H` |
| `#if` | Conditional compilation | `#if VERSION > 2` |
| `#else` | Alternative for #if | `#else` |
| `#elif` | Else-if for preprocessor | `#elif VERSION == 2` |
| `#endif` | End conditional block | `#endif` |
| `#pragma` | Compiler-specific instructions | `#pragma once` |
| `#error` | Generate compilation error | `#error "Not supported"` |
| `#line` | Change line number | `#line 100 "file.c"` |

### Include Syntax

| Syntax | Search Path |
|--------|-------------|
| `#include <file>` | System header directories |
| `#include "file"` | Current directory first, then system |

---

> **Key Point:** Preprocessor directives are processed before compilation. They perform text substitution, file inclusion, and conditional compilation.

quiz.question: What character starts all preprocessor directives?
